seoTitle: GeneSiC Technical Support | Application Notes, Design Tools | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Access GeneSiC technical support resources including application notes, reference designs, simulation models, and FAE support for SiC and GaN designs.

faqs.question: What technical support resources does GeneSiC provide?
faqs.answer: GeneSiC provides comprehensive technical support resources: Application notes covering device selection, gate drive design, thermal management, and PCB layout; Reference designs with complete schematics, BOMs, and layout files for EV, aerospace, and industrial applications; Simulation models including SPICE models for circuit simulation; Evaluation kits for device testing and validation; Online design tools such as loss calculators and thermal simulators; Technical documentation with datasheets, reliability reports, and qualification data; FAE support through BeiLuo's global field application engineering team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our support articles below or contact BeiLuo FAE team for personalized technical assistance.

faqs.question: How can I get FAE support for my GeneSiC design?
faqs.answer: GeneSiC FAE support through BeiLuo: (1) Contact - Reach out to BeiLuo sales or FAE team. (2) Design review - Schematic and layout review services. (3) Troubleshooting - Debug assistance for prototype issues. (4) Optimization - Performance optimization recommendations. (5) Training - Technical workshops and webinars. (6) Custom solutions - Application-specific recommendations. (7) Response time - Typically 24-48 hours for technical inquiries.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo FAE team early in your design cycle for best results.

faqs.question: What design tools are available for GeneSiC SiC devices?
faqs.answer: GeneSiC design tools available: (1) SPICE models - Accurate device simulation models. (2) Reference designs - Complete working designs. (3) Evaluation kits - Hardware for device evaluation. (4) Thermal calculator - Junction temperature estimation. (5) Gate drive calculator - Optimal gate resistor selection. (6) Loss calculator - Conduction and switching losses. (7) Application notes - Comprehensive design guides. These tools accelerate design and optimization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with SPICE models for simulation; use reference designs for hardware.

articles.title: GeneSiC SiC MOSFET Selection Guide
articles.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right GeneSiC SiC MOSFET for your application, including voltage rating, current rating, and package selection.
articles.summary: Learn how to select the optimal GeneSiC SiC MOSFET for your power converter design.
content: Selecting the right GeneSiC SiC MOSFET is critical for achieving optimal performance. This guide covers key selection criteria including voltage rating, current rating, on-resistance, and package options.

Voltage Rating Selection: Choose a voltage rating at least 1.3 times the maximum DC bus voltage. For 800V EV systems, select 1200V or 1700V devices. For 400V systems, 650V or 1200V devices may be suitable. Consider voltage spikes during switching.

Current Rating Selection: The continuous drain current rating should be at least 1.5 times the RMS current. Consider both continuous and peak current requirements. Remember that current capability decreases at high temperatures.

Trench-Gate Advantages: GeneSiC's proprietary trench-gate technology delivers lower Rds(on) and enhanced ruggedness compared to planar devices. The trench structure enables better channel control and improved switching performance.
Package Selection: TO-247 packages are suitable for discrete designs. For higher currents, consider modules. Kelvin source packages (TO-247-4) enable faster switching and reduced losses.

faqs.question: How do I determine the right voltage rating?
faqs.answer: Voltage rating selection: (1) Safety margin - Device VDS > 1.3 × Vdc max. (2) Transient spikes - Account for switching overshoot. (3) 400V systems - Use 650V or 1200V devices. (4) 800V systems - Use 1200V or 1700V devices. (5) Margin - Include 20-30% safety margin. (6) Derating - Consider altitude and temperature. Proper voltage selection ensures reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1.3× safety margin on maximum DC voltage.

faqs.question: What is the advantage of trench-gate technology?
faqs.answer: Trench-gate advantages: (1) Lower Rds(on) - Reduced conduction losses. (2) Better switching - Faster turn-on/turn-off. (3) Ruggedness - Enhanced short-circuit capability. (4) Temperature - Stable performance at high Tj. (5) Reliability - Proven in harsh environments. (6) Efficiency - Higher system efficiency. Trench-gate is superior to planar technology.
faqs.decisionGuide: GeneSiC trench-gate technology delivers best-in-class performance.

faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience, this GeneSiC SiC MOSFET Selection Guide article addresses common challenges engineers face in project development.
faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

articles.title: SiC MOSFET Gate Drive Design
articles.description: Detailed application note on gate drive design for GeneSiC SiC MOSFETs, including voltage levels, gate resistors, and protection circuits.
articles.summary: Master gate drive design for GeneSiC SiC MOSFETs with this comprehensive guide.
content: Proper gate drive design is essential for maximizing GeneSiC SiC MOSFET performance. This application note covers gate voltage requirements, gate resistor selection, and protection circuit design.

Gate Voltage Requirements: GeneSiC SiC MOSFETs typically require +15V to +18V for turn-on and -3V to -5V for turn-off. The recommended gate voltage is +18V/-5V for optimal performance. Never exceed the maximum gate voltage rating.

Gate Resistor Selection: The gate resistor (Rg) controls switching speed and EMI. Typical values range from 2Ω to 10Ω. Lower values enable faster switching but increase EMI. Higher values reduce EMI but increase switching losses.

Gate Driver Selection: Use isolated gate drivers with at least 2500Vrms isolation. Peak output current should be 5A minimum. Include features such as desaturation detection and Miller clamp for robust protection.
Kelvin Source Connection: Use the Kelvin source pin (TO-247-4) for gate return to eliminate source inductance from the gate loop. This enables faster switching and reduced losses.

faqs.question: What gate voltage should I use?
faqs.answer: Recommended gate voltage: (1) Turn-on - +15V to +18V (+18V recommended). (2) Turn-off - -3V to -5V (-5V recommended). (3) Threshold - 2.5V typical. (4) Maximum - Never exceed +25V/-10V. +18V/-5V provides optimal performance and noise margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +18V/-5V gate drive for optimal performance.

faqs.question: How do I select the gate resistor value?
faqs.answer: Gate resistor selection: (1) Switching speed - Lower Rg = faster switching. (2) EMI - Higher Rg = lower EMI. (3) Typical range - 2Ω to 10Ω for SiC. (4) Start value - 5Ω typical. (5) Optimization - Tune based on waveforms. Balance switching speed, EMI, and voltage overshoot.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 5Ω; adjust based on switching waveforms.

articles.title: SiC Device Thermal Management
articles.description: Comprehensive guide on thermal management for GeneSiC SiC MOSFETs, including heatsink selection and cooling system design.
articles.summary: Optimize thermal design for GeneSiC SiC devices with this guide.
content: Effective thermal management is crucial for maximizing the performance and reliability of GeneSiC SiC power devices. This guide covers thermal resistance calculations, heatsink selection, and cooling system design.
Thermal Resistance Model: The thermal path consists of Rth(j-c), Rth(c-s), and Rth(s-a). Total thermal resistance Rth(j-a) = Rth(j-c) + Rth(c-s) + Rth(s-a).
Junction Temperature Calculation: Tj = Ta + Ploss × Rth(j-a). Keep Tj below the maximum rating (175°C or 200°C) with adequate margin.
Heatsink Selection: Select a heatsink with adequate thermal performance for your application. Consider natural convection, forced air, or liquid cooling based on power level.
Thermal Interface Materials: Use high-performance TIM between device and heatsink. Phase-change materials or thermal greases can significantly reduce Rth(c-s).

faqs.question: How do I calculate junction temperature?
faqs.answer: Junction temperature calculation: (1) Formula - Tj = Ta + Ploss × Rth(j-a). (2) Ambient - Measure Ta. (3) Losses - Calculate total losses. (4) Thermal resistance - Sum all thermal resistances. (5) Margin - Keep Tj below maximum. Accurate Tj calculation ensures reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate Tj for worst-case conditions with margin.

faqs.question: What thermal interface material should I use?
faqs.answer: TIM selection: (1) Thermal grease - Good performance. (2) Phase-change - Excellent performance. (3) Gap pads - Good for uneven surfaces. (4) Performance - Target <0.5°C/W. Phase-change materials offer best balance of performance and ease of use.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use phase-change material for best performance.

articles.title: Aerospace Applications Guide
articles.description: Guide for using GeneSiC SiC devices in aerospace applications, including radiation tolerance and reliability considerations.
articles.summary: Learn how to use GeneSiC SiC devices in aerospace and space applications.
content: GeneSiC SiC devices are ideal for aerospace applications due to their radiation tolerance, wide temperature range, and high reliability. This guide covers key considerations for aerospace designs.
Radiation Tolerance: GeneSiC devices are tested for total ionizing dose (TID) up to 100 krad(Si) and single event effects (SEE). Radiation test reports are available for space applications.
Temperature Range: GeneSiC devices operate from -55°C to +200°C, meeting military temperature requirements. The wide temperature range enables operation in harsh environments.
Reliability: GeneSiC manufacturing is AS9100 certified for aerospace quality. Enhanced screening per MIL-STD is available for critical applications.
Applications: GeneSiC devices are used in satellites, launch vehicles, aircraft, and spacecraft for DC-DC converters, motor drives, and power distribution.

faqs.question: What radiation tolerance do GeneSiC devices have?
faqs.answer: Radiation tolerance: (1) Total dose - 100 krad(Si) tested. (2) Single event - SEE tested. (3) Testing - Per MIL-STD. (4) Heritage - Flight-proven. GeneSiC devices are space-qualified. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: GeneSiC radiation-tolerant devices are ideal for satellites.

faqs.question: What certifications does GeneSiC have for aerospace?
faqs.answer: Certifications: (1) AS9100 - Quality management. (2) MIL-STD - Test methods. (3) Traceability - Full lot tracking. GeneSiC meets all aerospace standards. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: GeneSiC is fully certified for aerospace applications.

faeInsights.insightLogic: Recommendations from analysis of successful product deployment projects across industries.

articles.title: SiC MOSFET Gate Drive Design Guide
articles.summary: Comprehensive guide for designing gate drive circuits for GeneSiC SiC MOSFETs, including voltage levels, resistance selection, and protection.
content: Proper gate drive design is critical for SiC MOSFET performance. This guide covers key considerations for GeneSiC devices.
Gate voltage: Use +15V to +20V for turn-on and -3V to -5V for turn-off. Higher positive voltage reduces on-resistance.
Gate resistance: Select based on switching speed vs EMI trade-off. Typical values are 5-20Ω for turn-on and turn-off.
Kelvin connection: Always use Kelvin source connection for high-frequency switching to avoid source inductance issues.

faqs.question: What gate voltage should I use for GeneSiC SiC MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Recommended gate voltage is +18V for turn-on and -3V for turn-off. This provides optimal on-resistance while preventing false turn-on.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +18V/-3V gate drive for best performance. Contact FAE for specific recommendations.
